deleteConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}?

disableUnconstrainedDelegation.title: Disable unconstrained Kerberos delegation
disableUnconstrainedDelegation.body:
This computer is trusted for unconstrained delegation: when a user authenticates against it, the computer receives a forwardable Ticket-Granting Ticket (TGT) for that user and can impersonate them to any other service in the domain.

Attack vector: an attacker who compromises this host harvests TGTs of every user that authenticated against it (cached for 10 hours by default) - including any Domain Admin who logged in - and replays them anywhere in the domain. This is the textbook 'golden ticket via TGT capture' primitive.

Recommended migration: replace with constrained delegation (set msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo to the specific target SPNs the host actually needs to call). Constrained delegation limits the blast radius to the explicit target services.

Risk if disabled blindly: legitimate Kerberos double-hop services WILL break. Examples include SQL Server linked servers running queries against another SQL instance under the calling user's identity, IIS web apps that impersonate the user to a back-end SQL or file share, and SharePoint with Kerberos delegation. Verify no production service on this host requires the current configuration before proceeding.
disableUnconstrainedDelegation.checkboxLabel: I have verified no production service on this host requires unconstrained delegation
